body: PT Jasnita Telekomindo Tbk (JAST) projects 20% revenue growth in 2024, driven by its subsidiary's expansion into Singapore, with potential projects offering significant opportunities to increase project volumes and expand service offerings. The company reported a net profit of IDR 218.7 million in Q3 2024, a recovery from a loss of IDR 3.2 billion in the same period last year, and is optimistic about exceeding its revenue targets in 2024 and 2025.
title: PT Jasnita Telekomindo projects 20% revenue growth in 2024 driven by Singapore expansion and new service offerings.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 3.14 billion shares in the Indonesian market, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) being the top sell with 552.02 million shares, followed by PT Kota Satu Properti Tbk (KOTA) with 105.52 million shares. On the other hand, PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (ASTR) topped the list of net buys with 85.12 million shares, followed by PT Buana Lintas Lautan Tbk (BULL) with 70.27 million shares.
title: Foreign investors sell 3.14 billion shares in Indonesian market, with PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK being the top seller.

body: PT Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Tbk (INKP) has acquired power plant assets from PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) with a total capacity of 175.1 MW, along with supporting equipment and inventories, in a deal classified as an affiliated transaction. The acquisition is not expected to have a significant impact on INKP's financial and operational conditions, according to Heri Santoso Liem, Director Corporate Secretary of INKP.
title: Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Acquires Power Plant Assets from Dian Swastatika Sentosa in Affiliated Transaction.

body: PT Mayora Indah Tbk (MYOR) plans to dissolve its Netherlands-based subsidiary, Mayora Nederland BV, which has been inactive since the end of its global medium-term note program in 1996 and still carries IDR 35 billion in liabilities. The liquidation is expected to have no impact on MYOR's business or financial position, and may even save operational costs, with the company reporting IDR 25.64 trillion in sales and IDR 2.06 trillion in net profit in September 2024.
title: PT Mayora Indah to dissolve its Netherlands-based subsidiary Mayora Nederland BV due to inactivity and liabilities.

body: The Jakarta Composite Index (JCI) closed flat at 7,083.28 points on Tuesday, largely unchanged from the morning session, due to declines in national banking companies such as Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) (-1.55%), Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) (-1.71%), and Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) (-1.32%). However, gains in stocks like Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (+7.65%), Barito Pacific Tbk (+1.53%), and Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk (+4.82%) helped sustain the index.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Remains Flat Amid Mixed Performance of Banking and Energy Stocks

body: PT Gesit Perkasa (no ticker) has increased its stake in PT Alakasa Industrindo Tbk (no ticker) to 84% by acquiring an additional 73.28 million shares at IDR 374 per share, totaling IDR 27.41 billion, significantly higher than the company's share price at the end of last year. As a result, PT Gesit Perkasa has consolidated its position as the primary shareholder, holding 84% of Alakasa's shares, with the remaining shares in public hands.
title: PT Gesit Perkasa increases stake in PT Alakasa Industrindo to 84% through IDR 27.41 billion share purchase.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.83 billion shares on Monday, January 6, with PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia (GOTO) experiencing the largest net sell of 1.43 billion shares, followed by PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA) and PT Kota Satu Properti Tbk (KOTA). Meanwhile, PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk (MBEN) saw the largest net buy of 71.82 million shares, followed by PT Perusahaan Gas Negara Tbk (PGAS) and PT Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA).
title: Foreign investors sell 1.83 billion shares in Indonesian market, with PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia experiencing the largest net sell.

body: PT Borneo Logistik Indonesia (BLI) sold 1.74 billion shares of PT Ancara Logistik Indonesia Tbk (ALII), representing 11.02% of ALII's total shares, for IDR 120.34 billion, with the purpose of debt repayment. The shares were immediately acquired by Borneo Logistics Services Pte Ltd (BLS) at the same price, with the goal of business development and investment.
title: PT Borneo Logistik Indonesia sells 11.02% stake in PT Ancara Logistik Indonesia to Borneo Logistics Services Pte Ltd for IDR 120.34 billion.

body: PT Hillcon Equity Management (Hill) raised IDR 103.6 billion by divesting 37 million shares of PT Hillcon TBK (Hill) at IDR 2,800 each, reducing its stake from 63.01% to 61.76%. The sale brings Hillcon Equity Management's total shares to 1.82 billion, with a free float rate of 398.42 million shares, and a total of 1,969 shareholders.
title: PT Hillcon Equity Management raises IDR 103.6 billion by divesting 37 million shares of PT Hillcon TBK.

body: Yos Effendi Susanto, the ultimate beneficial owner of PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk (PRAY), increased his stake by 3 million shares, raising his portion to 0.82% from 0.8% of the total listed shares, at a price of IDR 720 per share, totalling IDR 2.16 billion. As of December 31, 2024, PRAY's shareholders consisted of HSBC Fund SVS A/C with 27.15%, Yos Effendi Susanto with 0.8%, and others, with the company reporting a net profit of IDR 216.70 billion in September 2024, up from IDR 174.18 billion in Q3 2023.
title: Yos Effendi Susanto increases stake in PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk through personal transaction.

body: PT MNC Energy Investments Tbk (IATA) will issue 20.19 billion new shares worth IDR 50 each, with proceeds allocated towards working capital and business development, increasing its issued and paid-up capital to IDR 2.60 trillion. The rights issue, approved by shareholders on December 18, 2024, will take place from March 6-19, 2025, and will result in a stock portfolio worth IDR 4.57 trillion.
title: PT MNC Energy Investments to Issue 20.19 Billion New Shares to Raise Capital for Business Development.

body: Soul Parking, an Indonesian parking management service provider, has concluded its Series A+ funding round led by AppWorks and AC Ventures, with participation from other investors, although the amount raised remains undisclosed. The new capital will support Soul Parking's market improvement objectives and expansion plans to several countries, building on its mission to offer modern solutions to the parking industry in Indonesia.
title: Soul Parking concludes Series A+ funding round to support market improvement and expansion plans.

body: PT Rukun Raharja Tbk (RAJA) has received an "IDA+" rating from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia, citing its solid market position and secure contract scheme, which reflects its strong financial profile. The rating may be upgraded if RAJA boosts its inter-stream business, particularly in midstream and downstream segments, and maintains a solid financial profile, with the rating effective until December 1, 2025.
title: PT Rukun Raharja receives IDA+ rating from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia ahead of subsidiary's listing.

body: PT Multi Medika Internasional Tbk (MMIX) has formed a joint venture with Fujian Nicepaper Hygiene Products Co. from China, called PT Multi Nice Paper Indonesia, with an authorized capital of IDR 31 billion, where MMIX holds 15% shares and Fujian Nicepaper holds 85%. The joint venture will operate in the tissue paper and non-woven fabric industry, and is expected to positively contribute to MMIX's future business development strategy.
title: PT Multi Medika Internasional forms joint venture with Fujian Nicepaper to produce tissue paper and non-woven fabric.

body: PT Kereta Api Indonesia (none) refinanced its 2017 and 2019 shelf registration bonds with a total value of IDR 1.49 trillion, allocating 67% to principal debt of its 2017 bonds and 33% to principal debt of its 2019 bonds. The refinancing was done through the issuance of Shelf Registration Bond II Phase I 2024, worth IDR 1.5 trillion, with net proceeds of IDR 1.49 trillion after excluding public offering costs of IDR 7.48 billion.
title: PT Kereta Api Indonesia refinances IDR 1.49 trillion in bonds from 2017 and 2019 with new IDR 1.5 trillion issuance.

body: Four out of 10 coal companies with a market capitalization above IDR 25 trillion showed growth over the past year, led by PT Golden Energy Mines Tbk (GEMS) with an impressive 89.40% share growth, increasing from IDR 5,900 to IDR 11,175 per share. The other three companies that saw share price growth were PT Bayan Resources Tbk (BYAN) with a 2.38% increase, PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI) with a 25.25% increase, and PT Bukit Asam Tbk (PTBA) with a modest 0.75% gain.
title: Four major Indonesian coal companies see significant share price growth over the past year.

body: PT Bank Tabungan Negara Tbk (BBTN) has acquired two properties from PT Asuransi Jiwa IFG for IDR 15.9 billion, with the first property valued at IDR 10.81 billion and the second at IDR 5.09 billion, to support the bank's office relocation and expansion. The transaction, categorized as an affiliated transaction due to shared government ownership, does not involve a conflict of interest and is not considered material according to OJK regulations.
title: PT Bank Tabungan Negara acquires two properties worth IDR 15.9 billion to support office relocation and expansion.

body: Sucorinvest Equity Prima Fund sold 305.34 million shares of PT City Retail Developments Tbk (none), reducing its ownership from 6.38% to 5%, while PT Majukarya Mandiri Indonesia released 30.30 million shares of PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk (none), decreasing its share portion to 5.22%. Meanwhile, various investors, including CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd, PT Asuransi Jiwa Central Asia Raya, and individual investors, made notable transactions, buying and selling shares of companies such as PT Wilton Makmur Indonesia Tbk (none), PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (none), and PT Indika Energy Tbk (none).
title: Multiple investors engage in significant share transactions in various Indonesian companies on the first trading day of the new year.

body: PT United Tractors Tbk (UNTR) is injecting IDR 176.17 billion into its subsidiary PT Bina Pertiwi Energ (BPE) through another subsidiary, PT Energia Prima Nusantara (EPN), to meet BPE's working capital needs, increasing EPN's control over BPE to 391,499 shares. This affiliated transaction, set to conclude by December 31, 2024, demonstrates UNTR's commitment to promoting BPE's growth in the energy sector.
title: PT United Tractors subsidiary to acquire majority stake in PT Bina Pertiwi Energ through capital injection of IDR 176.17 billion.

body: PT Graha Prima Mentari Tbk (GRPM) secured a IDR 25 billion loan from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) to boost its working capital, with the lender having the flexibility to withdraw the loan facility through a request letter. GRPM's Q1 2024 net profit surged 64.09% year-on-year to IDR 3.61 billion, driven by a significant increase in sales to IDR 370.68 billion, and the company expects the extra credit to strengthen its financial position and support future business growth.
title: Graha Prima Mentari secures IDR 25 billion loan from Bank Mandiri to boost working capital.
